findings.title: Home entry CTAs bypass canonical spine and lose trace_id continuity

evidence.snippet: export function toHref(path: string, spine: QuerySpine, overrides?: ...)

impact.bettor: Trace continuity can drop at first click, weakening confidence that context is preserved.
impact.pm: Lifecycle attribution across home→product flow becomes noisier.
impact.eng: Duplicated URL composition logic raises regression risk.
impact.vc: Undercuts the single-spine product narrative in demos.

recommendation: Replace manual home CTA query-string composition with toHref(...).
Preserve trace_id in all home-originated product navigations.
Add a test asserting CTA hrefs carry full spine keys.

findings.title: Bettor-facing direct-link bypasses remain outside nervous routing helper

evidence.snippet: direct absolute href matches found in bettor-facing components

impact.bettor: Context can silently reset when moving between core surfaces.
impact.pm: Funnel and step-level continuity metrics fragment.
impact.eng: Repeated bypasses make spine enforcement brittle.
impact.vc: Weakens confidence in platform coherence claims.

recommendation: Route bettor-facing links through nervous.toHref/toHref plus appendQuery.
Allow explicit hard-reset links only with code comments documenting intent.
Expand tohref guard coverage for bettor/landing surfaces.

findings.title: ID contract drift: snake_case and camelCase are both emitted at API boundaries

evidence.snippet: returns trace_id and traceId in same response

impact.bettor: Potentially inconsistent run stitching across APIs and clients.
impact.pm: Ambiguous schema contracts complicate reporting specs.
impact.ux: Edge-case desync when consumers choose different field names.

recommendation: Standardize on snake_case IDs at external boundaries.
Convert to internal camelCase only within adapters, not API payloads.
Add tests/guards that reject camelCase ID keys in response contracts.

findings.title: DURING polling refetches full windows without incremental cursor support

evidence.snippet: GET supports limit only; no since/cursor

impact.bettor: Real-time feel can degrade under load.
impact.pm: Demo reliability and perceived responsiveness suffer.
impact.eng: Unnecessary dedupe/sort and repeated payload transfer.
impact.vc: Scalability concerns for concurrent active traces.

recommendation: Add since/cursor support to GET /api/events.
Update hook to consume deltas and append in order.
Apply adaptive polling/backoff based on visibility/idle.

findings.title: Shared chunk near-universality limits practical isolation and hurts first-fold efficiency

evidence.snippet: First Load JS shared by all 87.7 kB; / route first load 172 kB

impact.bettor: Slower initial page load can delay trust signals in first seconds.
impact.pm: Potential conversion drop on cold starts.
impact.eng: Harder to realize route-level code-splitting gains.
impact.vc: Performance story is weaker for scale-readiness.

recommendation: Audit root/shared dependencies and move non-critical code off hot path.
Increase dynamic/lazy loading for non-essential landing modules.
Gate CI with route-first-load and near-universal chunk thresholds.

findings.title: Governor allows known client/server boundary violations

evidence.snippet: knownViolations allowlist; tolerated boundary violations logged

impact.bettor: Boundary bugs can surface as unstable behavior in production-like flows.
impact.pm: Green governance checks may mask debt.
impact.ux: Client/server mixups can cause brittle rendering/runtime behavior.
impact.eng: Tolerances reduce urgency to close architecture gaps.
impact.vc: Governance confidence is reduced before v1.0.

recommendation: Remove each allowlisted violation with owner + deadline.
Fail CI once cleanup PRs land (no tolerated violations).
Report remaining exceptions as explicit release blockers.

findings.title: Legacy landing module tree appears orphaned from active route graph

evidence.snippet: legacy module imports from other legacy files
references found only within src/legacy subtree

impact.bettor: No direct runtime impact, but stale code raises long-term defect risk.
impact.pm: Slower audits and onboarding due to ambiguous canonical surface.
impact.ux: Risk of accidental reuse of outdated UI patterns.
impact.eng: Repo noise and maintenance overhead increase.
impact.vc: Deadweight can signal lower engineering hygiene.

recommendation: Verify no hidden runtime/test dependency on src/legacy/landing.
Archive or remove orphaned legacy modules.
Add periodic dead-code scans for src/legacy.

pr_plan.title: Spine continuity hardening across home + bettor surfaces
changes: Replace manual absolute href/query-string CTAs with toHref/nervous.toHref.
Add coverage in routing guard for bettor-facing directories.
Add focused tests on CTA spine preservation.

pr_plan.title: Boundary contract normalization to snake_case IDs
changes: Standardize API responses on trace_id/slip_id only.
Use adapters for internal camelCase mapping if needed.
Add route contract tests for today/events/slips endpoints.

pr_plan.title: Incremental DURING event retrieval
changes: Update useTraceEvents to fetch deltas and append in order.
